findings: The lungs are clear bilaterally without consolidation, mass, or nodule. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. The mediastinal structures are normal. Heart size is within normal limits. No lymphadenopathy. The osseous structures are unremarkable.

Solitary pulmonary nodule in the right upper lobe measuring 8 mm with smooth margins. No calcification within the nodule. Remainder of the lungs are clear. No pleural effusion. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

Bilateral pleural effusions, moderate on the right and small on the left. Associated compressive atelectasis at both lung bases. Scattered ground-glass opacities in the dependent portions of the lower lobes bilaterally. No focal consolidation or mass. Heart size mildly enlarged.

Filling defects within the right main pulmonary artery and bilateral lobar pulmonary arteries consistent with acute pulmonary embolism. Right ventricle appears dilated with RV/LV ratio greater than 1, suggesting right heart strain. Interventricular septum bowing toward the left ventricle. Small bilateral pleural effusions. No parenchymal infarct identified.

Extensive consolidation involving the right middle and right lower lobes with air bronchograms. Loculated right pleural effusion with enhancing pleural thickening consistent with empyema. Scattered consolidation in the left lower lobe. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy with subcarinal node measuring 15 mm in short axis. No pneumothorax. Endotracheal tube in satisfactory position.
